data.title: Perenti appoints South32 veteran Vanessa Torres as new CEO
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Perenti Global ( (AU:PRN) ) has provided an announcement.

Perenti has appointed Dr Vanessa Torres as its new managing director and chief executive, succeeding Mark Norwell as part of a planned leadership transition. Torres, a former South32 chief operating officer with more than 25 years of experience at major miners including BHP and Vale, will join the company in April and assume the top role in June, with Norwell staying on to support the handover.

The board said Torres was chosen after an extensive global search to build on the fourfold revenue growth and operational improvements delivered under Norwell’s tenure. Her appointment, supported by a substantial remuneration and incentive package tied to long- and short-term performance, is intended to advance Perenti’s strategy of developing a fully diversified portfolio and driving sustainable long-term growth for shareholders and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:PRN) stock is a Hold with a A$2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Perenti Global stock, see the AU:PRN Stock Forecast page.

More about Perenti Global

Perenti Limited is an ASX-listed, diversified mining services group providing contract mining, drilling services, mining services and technology solutions to the global resources industry. Headquartered in Perth and operating across four continents, the company focuses on delivering enduring value and certainty for investors, clients, employees and the communities where it operates.

Average Trading Volume: 3,068,393

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$1.8B

Vulcan Energy Resources Ltd. ( (AU:VUL) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Vulcan Energy Resources Limited has appointed Roberto Gallardo Fernandez Diez, known as Roberto Gallardo, as a director effective 1 April 2026. According to the initial director’s interest notice lodged with the ASX, Gallardo currently holds no relevant interests in the company’s securities and has no disclosed interests in related contracts, indicating a starting position free of shareholdings or contractual ties.

The appointment of a new director without existing equity or contract interests may underscore Vulcan Energy’s focus on governance transparency and independence at the board level. This clean-interest starting point could be significant for investors monitoring potential changes in strategic direction, oversight, and alignment of the board with shareholder interests as Gallardo assumes his role.

data.title: Wheaton Precious Metals Files 40-F and Sets May 8 Hybrid Shareholder Meeting
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Wheaton Precious Metals ( (TSE:WPM) ) is now available.

Wheaton Precious Metals Corp. announced on March 31, 2026 that it has filed its Form 40-F with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, making the report and its audited 2025 financial statements available on EDGAR and the company’s website. Shareholders can obtain printed copies of the audited financials free of charge upon request to the investor relations department.

The company also set May 8, 2026 for its Annual and Special Meeting of Shareholders, to be held in a hybrid format with an in-person venue in New York and a virtual platform that allows registered shareholders and proxyholders to listen and vote online in real time. The move underscores Wheaton’s push for broader shareholder participation and transparency as it reports on its 2025 performance and governance matters.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:WPM) stock is a Buy with a C$193.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Wheaton Precious Metals stock, see the TSE:WPM Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WPM is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by standout profitability and a fortress-like balance sheet, reinforced by a strongly positive earnings update with clear long-term growth targets. Offsetting factors are a premium valuation with a low dividend yield and a mixed near-term technical picture, plus noted variability in free cash flow conversion.

To see Spark’s full report on WPM stock, click here.

More about Wheaton Precious Metals

Wheaton Precious Metals Corp. is a leading precious metals streaming company, focusing on long-life, low-cost gold and silver assets. data.title: Mitesco Announces Brief Delay in Annual Filing
data.description:
Mitesco ( (MITI) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mitesco, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025 will be delayed. The late filing relates specifically to its annual report obligations for that period.

The company explains that it has not yet finalized its audited financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025. This internal timing issue is the key factor behind the delay, and there is no indication of auditor changes, investigations, or special committee reviews.

Mitesco states that it expects to file the delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on or before the fifteenth calendar day following the original due date. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual report to be submitted within this standard extension window.

The company does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year, based on information available at the time of the notice. As with all such statements, this expectation is forward-looking and could change once the audited financials are completed.

Mitesco affirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, underscoring its ongoing efforts to remain in compliance with SEC reporting rules. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer Brian Valania, dated March 31, 2025, signaling executive oversight of the disclosure process.

More about Mitesco

data.title: Wenyuan Group Delays Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Wenyuan Group ( (WYGC) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Wenyuan Group Corp. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The company, which operates from Hangzhou in China, is seeking brief relief from the standard reporting timetable while it completes required documentation.

The main reason given for the late filing is that Wenyuan Group has been unable to obtain all of the necessary financial information in time to finalize its annual report. Management states that filing the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on schedule would have required unreasonable effort or expense under these circumstances.

The company has indicated that it expects to submit the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard SEC extension window of up to 15 calendar days after the original deadline. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual report to be filed relatively soon, barring further unforeseen complications.

Wenyuan Group also notes that it does not expect any significant change in its operating results compared with the prior fiscal year to appear in the forthcoming earnings statements. Nonetheless, until the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed, all forward-looking indications about financial performance remain subject to revision and normal reporting adjustments.

The company confirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, supporting its overall compliance record. The notification is signed on behalf of Wenyuan Group Corp. by Chief Executive Officer Xizhen Ye, dated March 31, 2026, signaling management’s formal commitment to complete the delayed filing within the allowed extension period.

More about Wenyuan Group

Wenyuan Group Corp., formerly known as Longwen Group Corp., is based in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China. The company is listed in the U.S. and files periodic reports with the Securities and Exchange Commission, indicating a role in cross-border capital markets, although its specific line of business is not described in this filing.

data.title: Edgemode Delays 10-K Amid Larger 2025 Loss
data.description:
EdgeMode ( (EDGM) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Edgemode, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will delay its Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company says it cannot complete the filing on time without unreasonable effort or expense.

Management explains that it is still compiling the necessary financial information and facing challenges in preparing that data for the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). These internal preparation issues, rather than a regulatory or auditor dispute, are the stated cause of the late filing.

Edgemode expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the automatic extension period, specifically on or before the fifteenth calendar day after the original deadline. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual report to be submitted in mid-April, assuming no further delays.

The company is flagging a significantly larger net loss for 2025 than in 2024, driven mainly by a sharp rise in non-cash stock-based compensation and an impairment charge. Non-cash stock-based compensation alone is expected to be about $29 million in 2025, compared with zero in 2024, and the company cautions that all figures remain subject to completion of the audit and may change.

Edgemode notes that its other required SEC reports over the past 12 months have been filed, signaling ongoing baseline compliance despite the current delay. The notification is signed and certified on March 31, 2026, by Chief Financial Officer Simon Wajcenberg, underscoring management’s responsibility for both the late-filing notice and the forthcoming Form 10-K (Yearly Report).

More about EdgeMode

data.title: Capstone Holding Delays 2025 Annual Financial Report
data.description:
Capstone Holding ( (CAPS) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Capstone Holding Corp. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the year ended December 31, 2025. The company, which remains current on all other required filings, is postponing its annual report beyond the standard March 31, 2026 deadline for smaller reporting companies.

Management cites difficulties in finishing its financial statements and related disclosures as the main cause of the delay. Its independent registered public accounting firm also needs more time to complete the audit of the 2025 financial statements to be included in the Form 10-K (Yearly Report).

Capstone Holding Corp. expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) no later than 15 calendar days after the original due date, using the extension window allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual report to be filed by mid-April 2026, assuming no further complications arise.

The company is signaling that 2025 results will differ meaningfully from the prior year, largely because of two acquisitions: Fraser Canyon Holdings Inc. and Continental Stone Industries Inc. These deals required complex purchase price allocations and will introduce amortization of newly acquired intangible assets into the results.

Capstone is also finalizing its annual goodwill impairment analysis, which could lead to a material non-cash charge against earnings. On top of that, the company absorbed transaction and integration costs, and dealt with capital structure changes involving Series Z preferred stock and convertible promissory notes, adding further accounting complexity.

Because audit procedures are still ongoing and several items remain unsettled, Capstone Holding Corp. says it cannot yet reasonably estimate the quantitative impact of these factors on its 2025 results. All statements about expected filing timing and potential financial effects are forward-looking and subject to change as the audit concludes.

The company emphasizes that it intends to complete its Annual Report within the permitted extension period and continue meeting its SEC reporting obligations. The notification was signed on March 31, 2026, by Chief Executive Officer Matthew E. Lipman, underscoring executive-level oversight of the filing delay and remediation steps.

The most recent analyst rating on (CAPS) stock is a Hold with a $0.57 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Capstone Holding stock, see the CAPS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CAPS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CAPS is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance (ongoing losses and renewed cash burn), with bearish technicals reinforcing downside pressure. Corporate updates provide some offset via growth/monetization actions and reduced near-term costs, but listing-compliance risk and loss-based valuation keep the overall score low.

To see Spark’s full report on CAPS stock, click here.

More about Capstone Holding
